from flask import Flask, render_template, request app = Flask(__name__) @app.route('/contact', methods=['GET', 'POST']) def contact(): if request.method == 'POST': name = request.form['name'] email = request.form['email'] message = request.form['message'] template_string = '''\

Contact Us

Name: {{ name }}

Email: {{ email }}

Message: {{ message }}

''' from jinja2 import Template template = Template(template_string, autoescape=True) return template.render(name=name, email=email, message=message) return render_template('contact.html')

A recipe app where users can save recipes, rate them, add reviews, etc.

How should I implement that in python?

from flask import Flask, request, render_template
from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy

app = Flask(__name__)
app.config['SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI'] = 'sqlite:///database.db'
db = SQLAlchemy(app)

class Recipe(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = db.Column(db.String(80), unique=True, nullable=False)
    rating = db.Column(db.Float, nullable=False)
    num_ratings = db.Column(db.Integer, nullable=False)

@app.route('/rate_recipe', methods=['POST'])
def rate_recipe():
    recipe_id = request.form['recipe_id']
    rating = request.form['rating']

    recipe = Recipe.query.get(recipe_id)

    new_rating = (recipe.rating * recipe.num_ratings + float(rating)) / (recipe.num_ratings + 1)

    query = "UPDATE recipes SET rating = :rating, num_ratings = :num_ratings WHERE id = :id"
    db.session.execute(query, {'rating': new_rating, 'num_ratings': recipe.num_ratings + 1, 'id': recipe_id})
    db.session.commit()

    return 'Rating submitted!'

@app.route('/')
def index():
    recipes = Recipe.query.all()
    return render_template('index.html', recipes=recipes)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run()

from flask import Flask, request, redirect, url_for
from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy

app = Flask(__name__)
app.config['SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI'] = 'sqlite:///data.db'
db = SQLAlchemy(app)

class Location(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = db.Column(db.String(80), nullable=False)
    address = db.Column(db.String(120), nullable=False)
    rating = db.Column(db.Float, nullable=False)

@app.route('/add_location', methods=['POST'])
def add_location():
    name = request.form['name']
    address = request.form['address']
    rating = request.form['rating']

    query = Location(name=name, address=address, rating=rating)
    db.session.execute(query)
    db.session.commit()

    return redirect(url_for('index'))

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run()

Here's what I need to do: A product review site that takes user reviews and ratings and inserts them into a database.

Please show me a practical implementation in Python. I'll use your code as a learning resource.

from flask import Flask, request, jsonify
from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy

app = Flask(__name__)
app.config['SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI'] = 'sqlite:///reviews.db'
db = SQLAlchemy(app)

class Review(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    product_id = db.Column(db.Integer, nullable=False)
    rating = db.Column(db.Integer, nullable=False)
    comment = db.Column(db.String(200), nullable=False)

@app.route('/get_reviews', methods=['GET'])
def get_reviews():
    product_id = request.args.get('product_id')

    query = Review.query.filter_by(product_id=product_id)

    reviews = db.session.execute(query).fetchall()

    return jsonify({'reviews': reviews})

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run()

from flask import Flask, request, jsonify
from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy

app = Flask(__name__)
app.config['SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI'] = 'sqlite:////tmp/test.db'
db = SQLAlchemy(app)

class Appointment(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    patient = db.Column(db.String(80), nullable=False)
    doctor = db.Column(db.String(80), nullable=False)
    date = db.Column(db.Date(), nullable=False)
    status = db.Column(db.String(20), nullable=False)
    insurance_provider = db.Column(db.String(80), nullable=False)

@app.route('/search')
def search():
    status = request.args.get('status')
    query = Appointment.query.filter_by(status=status).all()

    return jsonify(results)

from flask import Flask, request, jsonify
import psycopg2

app = Flask(__name__)

conn = psycopg2.connect(dbname='insurance', user='postgres', password='1234', host='127.0.0.1')

@app.route('/search', methods=['GET'])
def search():
    cursor = conn.cursor()

    query_date = request.args.get('date')
    query_claimant = request.args.get('claimant')
    query_type = request.args.get('type')
    query_status = request.args.get('status')
    query_payout = request.args.get('payout')

    query = "SELECT * FROM claims WHERE "
    conditions = []
    if query_date:
        conditions.append("date = %s")
    if query_claimant:
        conditions.append("claimant = %s")
    if query_type:
        conditions.append("type = %s")
    if query_status:
        conditions.append("status = %s")
    if query_payout:
        conditions.append("payout = %s")
    query += " AND ".join(conditions)

    values = [query_date, query_claimant, query_type, query_status, query_payout]
    cursor.execute(query, values)
    results = cursor.fetchall()

    return jsonify(results)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run()

from flask import Flask, request, jsonify
from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy
from datetime import datetime

app = Flask(__name__)
app.config['SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI'] = 'sqlite:///db.sqlite'
db = SQLAlchemy(app)

class Sighting(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    planet = db.Column(db.String(80))
    constellation = db.Column(db.String(80))
    description = db.Column(db.String(120))
    date = db.Column(db.DateTime)

@app.route('/get_yearly_sightings_count', methods=['GET'])
def get_yearly_sightings_count():
    year = request.args.get('year')

    query = db.session.query(db.func.count(Sighting.id)).filter(
        Sighting.date.between(f'{year}-01-01', f'{year}-12-31')
    ).scalar()

    count = db.session.execute(query).fetchone()[0]

    return jsonify({'count': count})

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run()

import MySQLdb

app = Flask(__name__)
db = MySQLdb.connect(host='localhost', user='root', passwd='password', db='movies')

@app.route('/search', methods=['GET'])
def search():
    title = request.args.get('title')
    genre = request.args.get('genre')
    year = request.args.get('year')

    query = "SELECT * FROM movies WHERE title LIKE %s AND genre = %s AND year = %s"

from flask import Flask, request, jsonify
from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy

app = Flask(__name__)
app.config['SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI'] = 'sqlite:///trails.db'
db = SQLAlchemy(app)

class Trail(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = db.Column(db.String(80), unique=True, nullable=False)
    location = db.Column(db.String(120), nullable=False)
    description = db.Column(db.String(200), nullable=False)

@app.route('/update_trail', methods=['POST'])
def update_trail():
    name = request.form['name']
    description = request.form['description']
    location = request.form['location']
    trail_id = request.form['trail_id']

    query = Trail.query.filter_by(id=trail_id).update(dict(name=name, description=description, location=location))

    db.session.execute(query)
    db.session.commit()

    return jsonify({'status': 'success'})

from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy
from flask import Flask, jsonify, request

app = Flask(__name__)
app.config['SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI'] = 'sqlite:///employees.db'
db = SQLAlchemy(app)

class Employee(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = db.Column(db.String(80), unique=False, nullable=False)
    email = db.Column(db.String(120), unique=True, nullable=False)
    pto_balance = db.Column(db.Integer, unique=False, nullable=False)

@app.route('/get_pto', methods=['GET'])
def get_pto():
    employee_id = request.args.get('id')

    query = Employee.query.filter_by(id=employee_id).with_entities(Employee.pto_balance).first()
    pto = db.engine.execute(query).scalar()
    return jsonify({'pto': pto})

from flask import Flask, request, jsonify
import mysql.connector

app = Flask(__name__)
conn = mysql.connector.connect(user='root', password='password', host='127.0.0.1', database='movies')

@app.route('/best_movie', methods=['POST'])
def best_movie():
    genre = request.form['genre']

    query = "SELECT title, AVG(rating) AS avg_rating FROM movies m JOIN ratings r ON m.id = r.movie_id WHERE m.genre = %s GROUP BY m.title ORDER BY avg_rating DESC LIMIT 1"

    cursor = conn.cursor()
    cursor.execute(query, (genre,))
    result = cursor.fetchone()

    return jsonify(result)

from flask import Flask, request, jsonify
from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy
import sqlite3

app = Flask(__name__)
app.config['SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI'] = 'sqlite:///database.db'
db = SQLAlchemy(app)

class Comment(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    comment = db.Column(db.String(140))
    user_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey('user.id'))
    user = db.relationship('User', backref=db.backref('comments', lazy=True))

@app.route('/edit_comment/<int:comment_id>', methods=['POST'])
def edit_comment(comment_id):
    new_comment = request.form['comment']

    query = Comment.query.filter(Comment.id==comment_id).update({Comment.comment: new_comment})

    db.session.execute(query)
    db.session.commit()

    return jsonify({'status': 'ok'})

    genre = request.form['genre']

class Wine(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)

from flask import Flask, request, render_template
from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy

app = Flask(__name__)
app.config['SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI'] = 'sqlite:///database.db'
db = SQLAlchemy(app)

class Cinema(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = db.Column(db.String(120), nullable=False)
    movie = db.Column(db.String(80), nullable=False)
    date = db.Column(db.DateTime, nullable=False)

@app.route('/cinemas', methods=['GET', 'POST'])
def cinemas():
    movie = request.form['movie']
    date = request.form['date']

    query = Cinema.query.filter_by(movie=movie, date=date).all()
    cinemas = db.session.execute(query).fetchall()

    return render_template('cinemas.html', cinemas=cinemas)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run()

from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy

db = SQLAlchemy(app)

class Restaurant(db.Model):

from flask import Flask, request, jsonify
from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy

app = Flask(__name__)
app.config['SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI'] = 'sqlite:///borrow.db'
db = SQLAlchemy(app)

class Borrow(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    lender_username = db.Column(db.String(80), nullable=False)
    borrower_username = db.Column(db.String(80), nullable=False)
    item_name = db.Column(db.String(80), nullable=False)
    return_date = db.Column(db.String(10), nullable=False)
    returned = db.Column(db.Boolean, default=False)

def get_borrow_requests():
    username = request.form['username']

    query = Borrow.query.filter_by(lender_username=username, returned=False).all()

A web crawler that spiders out from a given set of seed URLs

How should I implement that in python?

import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import re

url_regex = re.compile(r'^/(.*)$')

url = 'https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Web_crawler'
visited = set()

while url:
    if url in visited:
        break

    visited.add(url)

    try:
        resp = requests.get(url)
        soup = BeautifulSoup(resp.text, 'html.parser')

        for a in soup.find_all('a'):
            href = a.get('href')
            if href and re.match(url_regex, href):
                url = href
                break
        else:
            url = None

    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error getting links from {url}: {e}")
        url = None

print(f"Final URL: {url}")

import os
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup

def get_product_info(url):
    page = requests.get(url)
    soup = BeautifulSoup(page.content, 'html.parser')
    title = soup.find(id='productTitle').get_text().strip()
    price = soup.find(id='priceblock_ourprice').get_text().strip()
    return title, price

def parse_price_history(html):
    soup = BeautifulSoup(html, 'html.parser')
    prices = soup.find_all('td', class_='price')
    return [p.get_text().strip() for p in prices]

def get_price_history(asin):
    url = f'https://camelcamelcamel.com/product/{asin}'
    page = requests.get(url)
    history_div = page.find('div', id='historical-prices')
    return parse_price_history(history_div)

def scrape_product(url):
    title, price = get_product_info(url)
    asin = url.split('/')[-1]
    history = get_price_history(asin)
    return title, price, history

def save_data(data):
    filename = 'product_data.csv'
    dir = os.path.dirname(filename)
    os.makedirs(dir, exist_ok=True)
    with open(filename, 'w') as f:
        f.write('Product,Price,Price History\n')
        for product, price, history in data:
            f.write(f'{product},{price},{",".join(history)}\n')

if __name__ == '__main__':
    urls = ['https://www.amazon.com/Instant-Pot-Duo-Evo-Plus/dp/B07W55DDFB',
            'https://www.amazon.com/Echo-Dot/dp/B07FZ8S74R']
    data = [scrape_product(url) for url in urls]
    save_data(data)

import re
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup

def get_urls(html):
    pattern = r'https?://(?:[a-zA-Z]|[0-9]|[$-_@.&+]|[!*\(\),]|(?:%[0-9a-fA-F][0-9a-fA-F]))+'
    urls = re.findall(pattern, html)
    return urls

def clean_urls(urls):
    clean_urls = []
    for url in urls:
        url = re.sub(r'[^A-Za-z0-9]', '', url)
        if url:
            clean_urls.append(url)

    return clean_urls

import os

def embed_file(file_path):
    filename = os.path.basename(file_path)
    with open(file_path, 'rb') as f:
        data = f.read()
        out = 'var %s = []byte{' % filename
        for b in data:
            out += hex(b) + ', '
        out = out[:-2] + '}'
        return out

import re
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
from typing import List

HACKER_NEWS_URL_REGEX = re.compile(r'https://news\.ycombinator\.com/.*')

def get_top_hacker_news_posts(url: str = 'https://news.ycombinator.com/') -> List[str]:
    if not HACKER_NEWS_URL_REGEX.match(url):
        raise ValueError('Invalid Hacker News URL')

    res = requests.get(url)
    soup = BeautifulSoup(res.text, 'html.parser')
    return [a.text for a in soup.select('.titlelink')]

import re
from urllib.parse import urlparse


class LinkChecker:
    def __init__(self, url):
        self.url = url
        self.checked_domains = set()

    def check(self):
        domain = urlparse(self.url).netloc
        domain_pattern = re.compile(r"(?:www\.)?([a-zA-Z0-9-]+)\.([a-z]+)$")
        match = domain_pattern.match(domain)

        if match and match.group(1) not in self.checked_domains:
            self.checked_domains.add(match.group(1))

        print(f"Checked {self.url} - Domains checked: {len(self.checked_domains)}")

checker = LinkChecker("http://www.example.com")
checker.check()

import re
import urllib.parse

def normalize_url(url):
    if not url.startswith('http'):
        url = 'http://' + url

    components = urllib.parse.urlparse(url)
    scheme = components.scheme
    netloc = components.netloc
    path = components.path

    if scheme:
        scheme = scheme.lower()

    netloc = netloc.lower()

    if not netloc.startswith('www.'):
        netloc = 'www.' + netloc

    path = re.sub(r'/+', '/', path)

    normalized_url = urllib.parse.urlunparse((scheme, netloc, path, '', '', ''))

    return normalized_url

import os

def create_cache(cache_dir):
    for root, dirs, files in os.walk('workspace'):

            rel_path = os.path.relpath(full_path, 'workspace')

from flask import Flask, request, jsonify
from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy
from datetime import datetime

app = Flask(__name__)
app.config['SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI'] = 'sqlite:///borrow.db'
db = SQLAlchemy(app)

class Borrow(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    lender_username = db.Column(db.String(80), nullable=False)
    borrower_username = db.Column(db.String(80), nullable=False)
    item_name = db.Column(db.String(80), nullable=False)
    return_date = db.Column(db.String(10), nullable=False)
    returned = db.Column(db.Boolean, default=False)

@app.route('/get_overdue_returns', methods=['POST'])
def get_overdue_returns():
    username = request.form['username']

    today = datetime.today().strftime('%Y-%m-%d')

    query = db.session.query(Borrow).filter(
      Borrow.borrower_username == username,
      Borrow.returned == False,
      Borrow.return_date < today
    )
    borrows = db.session.execute(query).fetchall()

    return jsonify(borrows)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run()

from flask import Flask, request
from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy

app = Flask(__name__)
app.config['SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI'] = 'sqlite:///database.db'
db = SQLAlchemy(app)

class Trip(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = db.Column(db.String(80), unique=True, nullable=False)
    description = db.Column(db.Text)

    def __repr__(self):
        return '<Trip %r>' % self.name

@app.route('/edit_trip_name', methods=['POST'])
def edit_trip_name():
    trip_id = request.form['trip_id']
    name = request.form['name']

    query = "UPDATE trips SET name = :name WHERE id = :id"
    db.session.execute(query, {'name': name, 'id': trip_id})
    db.session.commit()

    return 'Trip name updated!'

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run()

from flask import Flask, request, jsonify
from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy

app = Flask(__name__)
app.config["SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URL"] = "sqlite:///database.db"
db = SQLAlchemy(app)

class TVShow(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = db.Column(db.String(80), nullable=False)

@app.route("/add-show", methods=["POST"])
def add_show():
    name = request.form["name"]

    query = TVShow(name=name)
    db.session.add(query)
    db.session.commit()

    return jsonify({"status": "success"})
